projects.title: Unit 2 Project — Fraction Division in Action
projects.desc: Apply dividing fractions to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 2.

projects.title: Fraction Kitchen
projects.desc: Use fraction division to scale recipes and solve kitchen problems (6.NS.1).

projects.title: Master Chef Kitchen — Recipe Rescue (Part 2)
projects.desc: Interactive graphic novel: dividing whole and mixed numbers by fractions (6.NS.1).

title: Divide Mixed Numbers
contentObjective: I can divide mixed numbers by first changing them to improper fractions.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words mixed number, improper fraction, convert, reciprocal, and simplify.

turnAndTalk.question: Agent Chen maps a 3 1/2-mile route divided into 1/4-mile segments. Why can't she just divide 3 1/2 by 1/4 while it is still a mixed number?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A mixed number must first become an improper fraction so I can flip and multiply.

stems.en: I cannot divide yet because 3 1/2 is a ___ and I need an ___.
stems.es: No puedo dividir todavía porque 3 1/2 es un ___ y necesito una ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain that the mixed number must be converted to an improper fraction (7/2) before applying Keep, Change, Flip.
turnAndTalk.extend: Estimate the number of segments first. Is your estimate close to 14? Justify using the route length and segment size.
extendStems: Each mile holds ___ quarter-mile segments, so ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Walk your partner through how you changed the mixed numbers before dividing.
turnAndTalk.kernel: I converted each mixed number to an improper fraction, then used Keep, Change, Flip.

stems.en: First I converted ___ into the improper fraction ___.
stems.es: Primero convertí ___ en la fracción impropia ___.

stems.en: Then I flipped the divisor and got the quotient ___.
stems.es: Luego volteé el divisor y obtuve el cociente ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students correctly convert (multiply whole by denominator, add numerator) before flipping the divisor and simplifying.
turnAndTalk.extend: A student converted 2 1/3 to 6/3 instead of 7/3. Critique exactly what step they skipped.
extendStems: The correct improper fraction is ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: When would dividing mixed numbers come up in a real project, like building or cooking?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Whenever you split a mixed-number amount into fraction-size pieces.

stems.en: I would divide mixed numbers when ___.
For example, cutting ___ feet into ___-foot pieces gives ___.
stems.es: Por ejemplo, cortar ___ pies en piezas de ___ pie da ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students give a realistic project scenario and recognize the mixed-number amount must be converted first.
turnAndTalk.extend: Generalize the full set of steps for dividing any two mixed numbers, in order.
extendStems: My steps are: first ___, then ___, then ___.

vocabulary.definition: A whole number plus a fraction, like 2 1/3.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número entero más una fracción, como 2 1/3.
vocabulary.visual: 2 1/3 means 2 wholes and 1/3 more — picture 2 full circles and 1/3 of another
vocabulary.cloze: A number with a whole number and a fraction, like 2 1/3, is a ___.

examples.why: It has a whole part (2) and a fraction part (1/3).
That is an improper fraction, not a mixed number.

vocabulary.definition: A fraction where the top is bigger than or equal to the bottom, like 7/4.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una fracción donde el número de arriba es mayor o igual al de abajo, como 7/4.
vocabulary.visual: 7/3 is improper because 7 > 3. It equals 2 1/3 as a mixed number.
vocabulary.cloze: A fraction whose numerator is greater than or equal to its denominator, like 7/3, is an ___.

examples.why: The numerator 7 is greater than the denominator 3.
The numerator is less than the denominator, so it is a proper fraction.

vocabulary.definition: To change a number to a new form but keep the same value.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cambiar un número a otra forma sin cambiar su valor.
vocabulary.visual: 2 1/3 → multiply 2 × 3 = 6, add 1 → 7/3. Same value, different form.
vocabulary.cloze: Changing a mixed number into an improper fraction is to ___ it.

vocabulary.definition: To make a fraction smaller using the same parts, like 2/4 = 1/2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Hacer una fracción más pequeña con las mismas partes, como 2/4 = 1/2.
vocabulary.cloze: Writing a fraction in its smallest equal form is to ___ it.

vocabulary.definition: A fraction turned upside down. You use it in keep-change-flip.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una fracción volteada de arriba abajo. Se usa en deja-cambia-voltea.
vocabulary.visual: The reciprocal of 3/4 is 4/3. To divide by 3/4, multiply by 4/3.
vocabulary.cloze: To divide by a fraction, I multiply by its ___.

launch.narrative: Agent Chen is mapping a suspect's route. The total distance is 3 1/2 miles, and each segment between landmarks is 1/4 mile. She needs to know how many segments are on the route to place surveillance cameras at every landmark.
launch.contextImage: Detective pinning segment markers on a city map with string and pushpins
noticePrompts: What is the total distance of the route?
Will the number of segments be more or less than 3?
wonderPrompts: Why do we need to convert mixed numbers before dividing?
Can you divide mixed numbers without converting first?

explore.instructions: Convert each mixed number to an improper fraction, then use Keep, Change, Flip to divide.
explore.label: Convert and Divide Mixed Numbers

discourse.prompt: Why is converting to an improper fraction the first step when dividing mixed numbers?
discourse.sentenceFrame: Before dividing, I convert 2 1/2 to ___ because it is easier to ___ with improper fractions.

optional.stem: A recipe needs 1½ cups of flour. How many full recipes can you make with 6 cups?

approaching.label: Convert each mixed number to an improper fraction.

onLevel.label: Convert each mixed number to an improper fraction, then divide.
Match each mixed number division to its quotient.

onLevel.instructions: Use the bar model to show 2 1/2 ÷ 1/2. How many 1/2-size pieces fit into 2 1/2?
onLevel.label: Model 2 1/2 ÷ 1/2

onLevel.stem: A recipe makes 2 1/4 batches of cookies. Each batch uses 3/4 cup of butter. How many cups of butter are needed in total?
onLevel.explanation: This is multiplication: 2 1/4 × 3/4 = 9/4 × 3/4 = 27/16. Wait — re-read: we need 2 1/4 batches at 3/4 cup each, but the question for division practice should be: if you have 2 1/4 cups and each batch needs 3/4 cup, how many batches? 2 1/4 ÷ 3/4 = 9/4 × 4/3 = 36/12 = 3.

extending.title: Find the Mixed Number Error
extending.correctWork: 2 1/3 = 7/3 (not 6/3). Multiply: 2 × 3 = 6, then ADD the numerator 1 to get 7. So 7/3 × 2/1 = 14/3 = 4 2/3.
hints: Check the conversion: 2 × 3 = 6, then add the numerator 1 to get 7.
2 1/3 should convert to 7/3, not 6/3. The student forgot to add the numerator.

extending.prompt: A detective has a rope that is 5 1/4 feet long. She needs to cut it into pieces that are each 3/4 foot for tying evidence tags. How many pieces can she cut? Write the equation, show each step (convert, keep-change-flip, multiply, simplify), and verify your answer.
extending.sentenceFrame: First I convert 5 1/4 to ___. Then I use KCF: ___ × ___ = ___. The answer is ___ pieces. I can verify because ___ × 3/4 = 5 1/4.

extending.title: Find Liam's Mistake
extending.correctWork: Liam forgot to flip the second fraction. 7/2 ÷ 5/4 = 7/2 × 4/5 = 28/10 = 14/5 = 2 4/5.
hints: Look at Step 4. Did Liam flip the divisor before multiplying?
Keep-Change-Flip means the divisor 5/4 becomes 4/5. Liam multiplied by 5/4 instead of 4/5.

connect.scenario: A detective has a rope that is 5 1/4 feet long. She needs to cut it into pieces that are each 3/4 of a foot for tying evidence tags. How many pieces can she cut?
connect.promptQuestion: How does dividing mixed numbers help the detective?
connect.prompt: The detective can cut ___ pieces because 5 1/4 ÷ 3/4 = 21/4 × 4/3 = 84/12 = ___.
